ai-w8-t1.answer: AI API = HTTP endpoint you call with JSON (prompt in, text/json out). Same client skills as any REST API: headers, auth, timeouts.
ai-w8-t1.realWorld: OpenAI chat/completions — POST JSON, parse choices[0].message.content.

ai-w8-t2.answer: Your REST API serves **your** business data. AI API serves **model** responses. Both use HTTP — different contracts and billing.
ai-w8-t2.realWorld: GET /students/1 = your DB. POST to OpenAI = paid tokens per request.

ai-w8-t3.answer: Call from Java: HttpClient, RestTemplate, or WebClient. Set Content-Type, Authorization, read status, parse JSON with Jackson.

ai-w8-t4.answer: Prompt templates: reusable strings with {{variables}}. Keeps prompts consistent and out of business logic.

ai-w8-t5.answer: Request JSON: model name, messages array (role + content), temperature. Match provider docs exactly.

ai-w8-t6.answer: Parse response with POJOs or JsonNode. Check error field, not just 200 — providers embed errors in body.
ai-w8-t6.realWorld: Map to ChatResponse DTO; null-check nested fields before use.

ai-w8-t7.answer: Handle timeouts, retries with backoff, rate limits (429), and malformed JSON. Log request id, never log API keys.
ai-w8-t7.realWorld: 429 → exponential backoff; 401 → fix key, don't retry blindly.

ai-w8-t8.answer: AI drafts API descriptions from controller code. Verify paths, params, and examples match running app.
ai-w8-t8.realWorld: springdoc generates from annotations — AI polishes descriptions.

ai-w8-t9.answer: Swagger/OpenAPI from @Operation annotations. AI suggests descriptions; you keep truth in sync with code.
ai-w8-t9.realWorld: Wrong response schema in docs breaks client teams — diff after each API change.

ai-w8-t10.answer: AI suggests Postman tests: status, JSON fields, response time. You run collection in CI with Newman.
